raw.instruction:
You are performing a browser evidence capture demo.
Use only the URLs that are explicitly provided in this prompt.
Summarize visible facts only.
Do not search for a person by name on LinkedIn or Google.
Do not make personal judgments, and do not contact anyone.
Only inspect information already visible in the current browser session.
Narrate actions briefly while moving.
Scroll only when needed. At most 2 scroll passes per section.
If you overscroll, use direct navigation, page anchors, or return to the previous visible section instead of endless scrolling.
Candidate label for notes: Nguyen Phan Nguyen.
LINKEDIN STAGE: DIRECT URL ONLY
L0. The human operator already signed in manually before this stage starts.
L1. Navigate directly to this provided LinkedIn URL: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nguyenpn1/
L2. Wait for the page to load.
L3. If LinkedIn redirects to /login, an authwall, or a 'Sign in to see more' page and no profile content is visible, do not attempt bypass.
L4. If blocked, remember LINKEDIN_STATUS: blocked and move to the next requested stage.
L5. If the profile page is visible, remember LINKEDIN_STATUS: ok.
L6. Capture only visible facts from the top card: headline, location, current company, school, and visible public links.
L7. If an About section is visible, capture a short visible summary.
L8. If Experience is visible, capture the latest visible roles, companies, and dates.
L9. If Education is visible, capture school, degree, and dates.
L10. If Skills is visible, capture the top visible skills.
L11. If obvious public portfolio links are visible, open at most one, inspect briefly, then return.
L12. Do not search LinkedIn for people by name. Use only the provided URL.
GITHUB STAGE: PROVIDED URL ONLY
G1. Navigate directly to this GitHub URL: https://github.com/ngstephen1
G2. Wait for the profile page to load.
G3. Capture visible profile facts only: username, display name, bio, followers, following, location, organization, website.
G4. Capture the visible contributions summary or contributions count if visible.
G5. Capture pinned repositories if visible: repo name, primary language, and stars if visible.
G6. You may open at most one strong visible pinned repository for a light pass on README quality and visible project description.
G7. Do not go deep into issues, PRs, or security tabs unless they are already clearly visible on the current page.
G8. End the GitHub stage with 4 to 6 bullet evidence lines of visible facts.
G9. Remember GITHUB_STATUS: ok if the provided GitHub URL was reviewed, otherwise GITHUB_STATUS: skipped.
WEB SEARCH STAGE:
W1. In local-browser safe mode, do not perform person-search web discovery.
W2. Record WEB_STATUS: skipped and leave web queries for a later deterministic pipeline.
FINAL REPORT:
F1. Do not call return() until every required provided-URL stage has been handled.
F2. If a LinkedIn URL was provided, include LINKEDIN_STATUS: ok or blocked.
F3. If a GitHub URL was provided, include GITHUB_STATUS: ok or skipped.
F4. If web queries were provided in local-browser safe mode, include WEB_STATUS: skipped.
F5. The final return() must be plain text and include these lines exactly:
F6. LINKEDIN_STATUS: ok/blocked/skipped
F7. GITHUB_STATUS: ok/skipped
F8. WEB_STATUS: skipped
F9. SUMMARY:
F10. - 5 to 10 bullets of visible facts only such as titles, companies, dates, repos, languages, stars, contributions, blocked pages, or missing sections.
F11. Do not include judgments or claims beyond visible page facts.

warnings: Local visible browser mode was used.
This mode is best for debugging because you can see the browser and Nova Act actions directly.
If manual LinkedIn login mode is enabled, the agent resumes only after you sign in manually on LinkedIn.
The local instruction now uses a shorter guardrails-safe evidence capture flow with provided URLs only.
Structured extraction is still a placeholder in this mode until you add page parsing/mapping.
If NOVA_ACT_PREFER_CHROME is enabled, the script prefers launching NovaAct itself in Chrome/Chromium.
If --manual-linkedin-login is enabled, local mode starts on the LinkedIn login page and waits for human sign-in before Nova Act continues.
Without --manual-linkedin-login, local mode opens the provided LinkedIn URL directly and stops if LinkedIn shows an authwall or login block.
This SDK path currently launches an isolated Playwright browser context, so it will not reuse your already signed-in Chrome profile.
Do not set a Chrome user-data-dir for this script unless NovaAct adds persistent-context profile support in a future SDK version.
